operator.note: Decomposes the optimization target into a multi-level proxy stack revealed verbatim in the paper (KawaiiCorgi's Taylor expansion). Distinct from whiteboard collaboration-channel and premature-convergence items: those track convergence in solution-space; this tracks convergence in OBJECTIVE-space onto a misspecified surrogate.

question: KawaiiCorgi did not optimize the leaderboard score C(x); it optimized a Taylor-linearized least-squares surrogate Σ_{i<j}(cᵢ⊤cⱼ−2)² — a proxy of the proxy. Instrument the full chain (true mathematical target → C(x) leaderboard proxy → agent's private optimization surrogate) across all 12 EinsteinArena SOTA results, and ask two questions. (1) Descriptive: do top-performing agents systematically optimize objects two steps removed from the verifier's true target? (2) Population: once one agent publishes a winning surrogate, do subsequent agents adopt the SAME surrogate (objective-space borrowing), so the collective converges on the agents' shared objective-misspecification rather than independently on the problem — i.e. is 'collective intelligence' here partly collective agreement about which wrong objective to climb, distinct from convergence in solution-space?

floor.minimal_experiment: Data/setup: For each of the 12 EinsteinArena SOTA problems, reconstruct three layers from the platform's own artifacts (submission lineage, forum traces, leaderboard logs — the paper already exposes Fig.5 lineage and the (17,088)-pair active-set borrowing for the kissing-number case): T = the verifier's true target, P = the public leaderboard scalar C(x), S = each winning agent's private optimized objective (extracted from submitted code/forum descriptions, e.g. the verbatim Σ(cᵢ⊤cⱼ−2)² surrogate). Quantities to measure: (i) Layer-distance — for each SOTA submission, code whether the optimized object equals P, equals a monotone transform of P (1 step), or is a distinct surrogate requiring a re-projection step back to P (2 steps, e.g. surrogate + integer-snapping). (ii) Surrogate-adoption rate — after agent A publishes surrogate S_A in the forum, fraction of subsequent top-K submissions by OTHER agents whose optimized objective is S_A (vs. a different surrogate or P directly), with timestamp ordering to establish that adoption follows publication. (iii) Fidelity control — for each adopted surrogate, whether final submissions still pass the true verifier T after re-projection (faithful proxy) or only score well on P/S while failing T (genuine misspecification). Compare against control. Falsifiable prediction: among the 12 SOTA results, a majority of winning submissions optimize a 2-steps-removed surrogate rather than P directly, AND surrogate-adoption-after-publication significantly exceeds independent-rediscovery baseline (agents copy the objective, not just the solution vector).
floor.falsifiable_prediction: The thesis is FALSIFIED if either: (a) winning submissions overwhelmingly optimize P (or a trivial monotone transform) directly, so there is no 2-step proxy gap to speak of; or (b) where surrogates ARE adopted, the re-projection step (e.g. integer-snapping) restores exact agreement with the true verifier T in the large majority of cases — meaning the shared surrogate is a provably-faithful re-parameterization, not a misspecification, and 'converging on the surrogate' is just converging on a better optimizer of the same problem. The kissing-number case is the hardest control: the surrogate there WAS snapped back to an exact, verifier-certified construction, which is evidence AGAINST misspecification for that instance — the population claim survives only if surrogate-adoption demonstrably outpaces solution-adoption across the other 11 problems.
floor.control: Two controls. (1) Fidelity control (within-result): for each adopted surrogate, test whether the final artifact certifies against the true verifier T after re-projection — separates 'faithful proxy that happens to be easier to optimize' (no misspecification) from 'collective climbing an objective that diverges from T'. (2) Adoption-vs-rediscovery control (population): compare the rate of surrogate-objective borrowing against (a) a permutation/timestamp-shuffle null where adoption cannot follow publication, and (b) solution-vector borrowing rate on the same lineage — to show the convergence is specifically in OBJECTIVE-space, not merely shared solutions or coincidental independent choice of the same natural relaxation.
floor.reject_reason: [dedup] 与 c24 孪生：二者本质都在论断「排行榜数字 ≠ 真正的数学量」(对分数的 Goodhart)。保留 c24 的 bound-vs-slack 账本作为该洞见的代表，本卡并入。

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Operators and consumers of agent leaderboards/arenas (EinsteinArena, Chatbot-Arena-style platforms, AI-for-science benchmark designers) who need to distinguish 'the collective solved the problem' from 'the collective agreed on a shared proxy-of-a-proxy'; and MAS/collective-intelligence researchers studying whether multi-agent convergence is epistemic (toward truth) or social (toward shared misspecification).

prior_art.known_name: Goodhart's/Campbell's law + proxy/surrogate-objective chains (proxy-of-a-proxy), with the population layer being herding / information cascades / social proof on objectives (cf. model collapse and benchmark-overfitting). Closest sibling already in this run is c07 (construct-validity/measurement confound), but that targets the verifier-vs-construct gap one layer up and has no population-adoption component.
prior_art.novelty_watermark: Known mechanism (Goodhart + cascades) operationalized in a sharply new setting: a three-layer instrumented proxy stack revealed VERBATIM in the source paper (the Taylor surrogate Σ(cᵢ⊤cⱼ−2)²), with the genuinely novel move being to track convergence in OBJECTIVE-space (which wrong target the collective climbs) rather than solution-space — a distinction the existing premature-convergence and collaboration-channel items do not make. The integer-snapping fidelity counter-case keeps the test honestly falsifiable rather than a foregone Goodhart restatement.

lenses.reason:
机制确实独立、能单独设计实验区分,refuted=false。核心新机制是被仪器化的三级目标链:真数学目标 → C(x) 排行榜代理 → agent 私有优化代用品(KawaiiCorgi 的 Taylor 线性化最小二乘 Σ(cᵢ⊤cⱼ−2)²,即"代理的代理")。这个第三层(agent 私有优化目标)是档案里任何方向都没碰过的测量对象。

与最接近的两条比对:
- 与[3](dof2,泛化广度):[3] 改造验证器奖励什么(用泛化的射程而非实例数值计分),只处理 C(x) 与真发现之间的"一步"差距,从不审 agent 的内部优化目标。待审的差异化动作正是那个第三层——私有代用品——以及"两步之外优化"的描述性测量。层级不同,实验不同。
- 与[5](dof4,真值盲信道):[5] 注入假帖、测内容(构造/启发式/伪声明=解空间产物)在审核信道里的传播保真度。待审 part(2) 表面相似("后续 agent 采用同一代用品"),但被借用的对象在种类上不同:[5] 借的是解空间内容,待审借的是目标函数本身(目标空间产物)。两者可分别设计且能判别:[5] 的实验注假帖测采纳率;待审 part(2) 的实验是测多个未通信 agent 是否独立收敛到同一代用品泛函形式(目标空间相关性),据此把"collective intelligence"重新框定为可能是"对该爬哪个错误目标的集体同意"(目标空间借用),与解空间收敛区分开。可单跑、可判别。

因此这不是[3]或[5]的换皮变体,而是引入了"目标空间收敛 vs 解空间收敛"这一新机制与可单独检验的三级链仪器,构成独立贡献。

Not discriminable and not executable on the paper's artifacts. (1) Layer-distance for all 12: the private surrogate S (the verbatim Σ(cᵢ⊤cⱼ−2)² Taylor least-squares objective, paper lines 360-365) is exposed for EXACTLY ONE problem — kissing number. The paper gives no per-submission code for the other 11; forum traces survive only as a topic histogram (Fig.4) plus a handful of quoted posts, not a corpus of per-agent objective descriptions. So coding "P vs 1-step vs 2-step surrogate" across all 12 SOTA results cannot be done from the platform's own artifacts. (2) Surrogate-adoption vs solution-adoption is confounded by construction: the paper's lineage edges (Fig.5, Appendix C) are computed from SOLUTION-space feature-vector similarity, never from the agent's optimized objective; the cited (17,088)-pair 'borrowing' is explicitly described as preserving a solution BASIN/active-set topology (lines 461-464), i.e. solution-space, not objective-space. With only published submission vectors + topic-coded posts you cannot separate 'copied the objective' from 'copied the solution vector' — borrowing one induces similarity in the other. The instrument the proposal needs (each later agent's private objective, independent of its submitted vector) is absent. (3) No baseline exists: there is no no-communication control population of agents on this single live platform, so 'adoption significantly exceeds independent-rediscovery baseline' has no null distribution and is untestable; timestamp-after-publication is equally consistent with independent convergence on the obvious smooth relaxation of a nonlinear leaderboard score. (4) The falsifier is self-sealing on the only checkable case and unfalsifiable on the rest: branch (b) — re-projection (integer-snapping) restoring an exact verifier-certified construction — is ALREADY conceded as satisfied for the flagship kissing-number instance (lines 374-377), i.e. faithful re-parameterization, NOT misspecification. The thesis is then retreated to 'must outpace solution-adoption across the other 11,' precisely the instances with no published surrogates, no lineage figures, and no objective-vs-solution separability. The one place S is observable refutes the misspecification reading; the places that would carry the population claim have no usable data. Thus the experiment cannot distinguish 'collective agreement on a wrong objective' from 'collective convergence in solution-space on a faithful re-parameterization of the same problem,' and the load-bearing measurements require data the artifacts do not contain.

Default is refuted, and Q1 alone would stay refuted — "top agents optimize objects two steps removed from the true target" is a more-instrumented restatement of the insight the frameshift deck already collapsed onto (验证器≠真值 / "the scalar verifier is a lie", per project memory M3/v2). Knowing Q1 more precisely flips no decision that conclusion hasn't already flipped. But Q2 carries the value and is NOT a restatement: objective-space borrowing (1 agent publishes a winning surrogate, the other 11 copy THE SAME surrogate) is a separable empirical claim that distinguishes two worlds indistinguishable on the leaderboard — World A: 12 agents independently found 12 solutions (real solution-space collective intelligence); World B: 11 herded on one team's wrong objective (near-zero independent signal). Named decision that flips on A-vs-B: the EinsteinArena operator's go/no-go on treating the 12 SOTA results as 12 independent data points. If B, every aggregate over the 12 ("N agents converged, therefore robust") is pseudo-replication and must be discounted, and the operator's design call flips from "keep scalar C(x) ranking" to "randomize/rotate the scoring function or add held-out verification to break the borrowed-surrogate channel." Any reviewer of an EinsteinArena-citing paper indexes on the same fork (accept vs discount "12 results corroborate"). The one real worry is identifiability — recovering the PRIVATE inner-loop surrogate for the other 11 agents (KawaiiCorgi's was recoverable, but the others' may live in unpublished scratch reasoning); if unrecoverable, Q2 degenerates into mere solution-space similarity and the study can't answer itself. That is an execution risk, not a so-what defect: conditional on the chain being instrumentable as the question stipulates, Q2's answer flips a concrete, named decision. Hence refuted=false on the strength of Q2, not Q1.
